response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with genuine systems-programming instincts, but the fit for this specific role is weak. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments ending in 2015; the last decade has been dominated by TypeScript/Node.js product work and DLang. The role demands current, architect-level C++/STL expertise plus industrial-domain depth (MQTT, IoT, IEC 62443 SDL, industrial protocols), none of which appears on the resume. His low-latency DLang work and mentoring record are credible transferable signals, but not enough to offset the core stack and domain gaps.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic, handling millions of requests per second
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing record: workshops, courses, refactoring dojos, and conference talks
Broad full-stack and DevOps versatility with event-driven architecture exposure (GISCollective platform)
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal (~7 months) and roughly a decade stale; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity, SDL, IEC 62443, MQTT, or IoT/industrial-automation protocol experience
No demonstrated Azure or message-broker-at-scale work matching the role's on-prem/cloud platform focus

key_factors.factor: Systems-level performance engineering and mentoring ability
